Supporting Information 3

Daniel Y.M. Nakamura, Ward C. Wheeler, Taran Grant

2025-09-14

Phylogenetic analyses of extant taxa have relied mostly on DNA sequences. The proportion of phenomic characters relative to molecular characters has been reduced with the advent of massive parallel DNA sequencing. However, phenomic evidence enables sampling taxa without molecular data (such as fossils). Here we examine the impact of phenomic evidence on phylogenetic analyses dominated by DNA sequences. We answer five questions using 57 empirical data sets (22 with morphology-only taxa). First, are topologies impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence in TE trees relative to MOL trees? Second, are distances between MOL and TE topologies associated with the proportion of phenomic characters? Third, is the addition of phenomic evidence equally impactful on ML and MP topologies? Fourth, how do bootstrap values of shared clades differ between MOL and TE trees? Fifth, can we retrodict the occurrence (i.e. monophyly) of TE groups based on bootstrap values of MOL clades? These questions were assessed with and without fossils.

PRE-AMBLE

Q1. Are topologies impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence?

Dataset with MOL-TE topological distances were calculated in script 1.

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")

print("EXTANT: Number of datasets (extant) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence")
## [1] "EXTANT: Number of datasets (extant) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence"
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (number)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (number)"
sum(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 50
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)"
sum(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 
## [1] 0.877193
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)"
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 50
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)"
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 
## [1] 0.877193
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC"
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 49
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)"
sum(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extant) 
## [1] 0.8596491
print("EXTINCT: Number of datasets (extinct) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence")
## [1] "EXTINCT: Number of datasets (extinct) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence"
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (number)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (number)"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 21
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
## [1] 0.9545455
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)"
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 18
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)"
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
## [1] 0.8181818
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC"
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 18
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)"
sum(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_extinct) 
## [1] 0.8181818
print("EXTANT: Number of datasets (extant keeping the same datasets from extinct) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence")
## [1] "EXTANT: Number of datasets (extant keeping the same datasets from extinct) impacted by the addition of phenomic evidence"
df_filtered <- df[df$Dataset %in% df_extinct$Dataset, ]
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (number)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (number)"
sum(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 20
print("MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)")
## [1] "MP MOL vs MP TE (proportion)"
sum(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_filtered) 
## [1] 0.9090909
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (number)"
sum(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 20
print("ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE ASC (proportion)"
sum(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_filtered) 
## [1] 0.9090909
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC"
sum(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) 
## [1] 20
print("ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)")
## [1] "ML MOL vs ML TE noASC (proportion)"
sum(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF > 0) / nrow(df_filtered) 
## [1] 0.9090909

Considering extant taxa only (n = 57), the phenomic evidence impacted topologies in 87.7% (50/57) of the datasets in MP and ML (MKv with bias correction) and 85.9% (49/57) in ML (MK without bias correction). Considering both extant and extinct taxa (n = 22), the phenomic evidence impacted topologies in 95.5% (21/22) of the datasets and 81.8% (18/22) in ML (both in MKv and MK). Considering a reduced dataset of extant taxa (only extant taxa for these 22 datasets where extinct taxa are available), the phenomic evidence impacted topologies in 90.1% (20/22) of the datasets in MP and ML (both in MKv and MK).

Q1.1 Data exploration

Q1.1.1 Extant (Table 1)

Considering extant taxa (n = 57; see Table 1):

print("1.a MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "1.a MP MOL vs MP TE"
print("No. shared clades")
## [1] "No. shared clades"
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
## [1] 44.84211
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
## [1] 52.64768
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
## [1]   6 254
print("No. unique MOL clades")
## [1] "No. unique MOL clades"
mean(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
## [1] 5.666667
sd(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
## [1] 6.415198
range(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesMOL)
## [1]  0 24
print("No. unique TE clades")
## [1] "No. unique TE clades"
mean(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
## [1] 7.122807
sd(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
## [1] 8.697434
range(df_extant$MP_nUniqueCladesTE)
## [1]  0 37
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## [1] 0.1635157
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## [1] 0.1499367
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## [1] 0.0000000 0.6666667
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
mean(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## [1] 0.1464907
sd(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## [1] 0.1351113
range(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## [1] 0.0000000 0.7269341
print("Mean SPR")
## [1] "Mean SPR"
mean(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
## [1] 0.1129238
sd(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
## [1] 0.08132133
range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
## [1] 0.0000000 0.3739288
print("Min SPR")
## [1] "Min SPR"

## [1] 0.07707913

## [1] 0.07804406

## [1] 0.0000000 0.3739288

## [1] "Resolution MOL"

range(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))

Q1.1.3 Reduced (Table S10)

Considering only extant taxa in datasets where extinct taxa are available (n = 22):

print("1.a MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "1.a MP MOL vs MP TE"
print("No. shared clades")
## [1] "No. shared clades"
mean(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_nSharedClades)
## [1] 42.31818

## [1] 48.31975

## [1]   7 234

## [1] "No. unique MOL clades"

## [1] 6.954545

## [1] 7.033778

## [1] "RF"

## [1] "CID"

## [1] 0.1394909

## [1] 0.0000000 0.3906157

## [1] "Mean SPR"

## [1] 0.1225725
sd(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))
## [1] 0.08621348

## [1] 0.0000000 0.3217293

## [1] "Min SPR"

## [1] 0.09125174

## [1] 0.07863722
range(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR))
## [1] 0.0000000 0.3217293
print("Resolution MOL")
## [1] "Resolution MOL"
mean(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
## [1] 1.5
sd(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
## [1] 2.558832
range(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_nPolytomiesMOL))
## [1]  0 11
print("Resolution TE")
## [1] "Resolution TE"

## [1] 1.863636
sd(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
## [1] 2.315485
range(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_nPolytomiesTE))
## [1]  0 10

Q1.2 Statistical tests

Q1.2.1 Extant (Table S4)

Considering extant taxa only, there are 50 datasets in which MOL-TE incongruences are not equal to zero and 7 datasets in which they are equal to zero in MP and ML (with MKv). In ML (with MK, no bias correction), there are 49 datasets in which MOL-TE incongruences are not equal to zero and 8 datasets in which they are equal to zero. To test if MOL-TE incongruences are significantly different from zero, we can check if the distribution of observed values are significantly different from zero.

For each comparison and for each topological distance metric, we tested for normality. Because data are not normal according to Shapiro-Wilk tests (P < 0.05 in all cases), we tested if the median of each topological distance metric is different from zero using a non-parametric Wilcoxon Signed-Ranked test. First, the V statistic is computed by summing the signed ranks of the differences between the observed values and the hypothesized value (i.e. zero). The ranks are assigned based on the absolute values of the differences, and the sign is determined by whether the original value is positive or negative. Second, given that the distribution of V values is normally distributed for a large sample size due to the Central Limit Theorem, P-values can be calculated by finding the area under the normal curve corresponding to the calculated Z-score.

print("1a. MPmol vs MPte")
## [1] "1a. MPmol vs MPte"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.8517, p-value = 5.485e-06
q1a_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.787e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1320493 0.1993178
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1640653
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))  # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.8285, p-value = 1.241e-06
q1a_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.79e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1162186 0.1763662
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1404831
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.94641, p-value = 0.01357
q1a_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_spr
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.789e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1030905 0.1438049
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1217251
print("1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc")
## [1] "1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.81265, p-value = 4.805e-07
q1b_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.783e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1257949 0.1880973
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1520081
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.80857, p-value = 3.793e-07
q1b_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.79e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09049794 0.13460595
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1108879
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.93234, p-value = 0.003357
q1b_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_spr
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## V = 1275, p-value = 7.787e-10
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09613346 0.13655240
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1163737
print("1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc")
## [1] "1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.8073, p-value = 3.525e-07
q1c_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 1225, p-value = 1.145e-09
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1215772 0.1883656
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##       0.148519
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.78489, p-value = 1.016e-07
q1c_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## V = 1225, p-value = 1.145e-09
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.08905922 0.13440579
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1083645
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.88833, p-value = 7.608e-05
q1c_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_spr
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## V = 1225, p-value = 1.145e-09
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09228541 0.13453552
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1126816

In all cases, the median of normalized RF, CID, and SPR values in MP and ML are significantly different from zero. Thus, MOL-TE incongruences different from zero indicate that the phenomic evidence tends to impact topologies in empirical studies.

Q1.2.2 Extant + extinct (Table S5)

Now, let’s test the same for the 22 data sets including fossils.

print("1a. MPmol vs MPte")
## [1] "1a. MPmol vs MPte"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.81198, p-value = 0.0007751
q1a_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## V = 231, p-value = 6.403e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1602766 0.4355901
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.2500777
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))  # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.83448, p-value = 0.001826
q1a_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## V = 231, p-value = 6.403e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1623873 0.4371822
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.2582056
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR)
## W = 0.86182, p-value = 0.005534
q1a_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_spr
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## V = 190, p-value = 0.000143
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.07898347 0.14531586
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1073432
print("1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc")
## [1] "1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.86152, p-value = 0.005463
q1b_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 171, p-value = 0.0002137
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09965938 0.25007044
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1660928
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.9104, p-value = 0.04824
q1b_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## V = 171, p-value = 0.0002141
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.07629423 0.17553359
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1254012
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # parametric, p > 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.92721, p-value = 0.1073
q1b_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1b_spr
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## t = 5.5097, df = 21, p-value = 1.818e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.06481411 0.14340641
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
## 0.1041103
print("1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc")
## [1] "1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.8607, p-value = 0.00528
q1c_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 171, p-value = 0.000213
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1004946 0.2456374
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1633758
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.90702, p-value = 0.04117
q1c_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## V = 171, p-value = 0.0002141
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.0755968 0.1694951
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1250558
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.91621, p-value = 0.06347
q1c_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1c_spr
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## t = 5.2591, df = 21, p-value = 3.259e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.06228581 0.14376568
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
## 0.1030257

Q1.2.3 Reduced (Table S11)

Now, let’s test the same for the 22 data sets including fossils.

print("1a. MPmol vs MPte")
## [1] "1a. MPmol vs MPte"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.89408, p-value = 0.02267
q1a_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.569e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1071282 0.2414787
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1671265
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))  # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.9049, p-value = 0.03729
q1a_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.569e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.0960722 0.2021012
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1382446
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMinSPR)
## W = 0.90588, p-value = 0.03903
q1a_spr <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1a_spr
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.569e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09575925 0.17138269
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##       0.131675
print("1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc")
## [1] "1b. MLmol vs MLteAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.87199, p-value = 0.008527
q1b_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.556e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.09467014 0.21954757
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1433845
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.93388, p-value = 0.1478
q1b_cid <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1b_cid
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.569e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.07299149 0.14752829
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##       0.106402
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # parametric, p > 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.94191, p-value = 0.2167
q1b_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1b_spr
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## t = 6.1638, df = 21, p-value = 4.085e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.07171519 0.14474734
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
## 0.1082313
print("1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc")
## [1] "1c. MLmol vs MLteNoAsc"
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## W = 0.86482, p-value = 0.00628
q1c_rf <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF), mu = 0, exact=F, conf.int=T)
q1c_rf
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
## V = 210, p-value = 9.556e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.08980276 0.21954757
## sample estimates:
## (pseudo)median 
##      0.1392216
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## W = 0.93466, p-value = 0.1534
q1c_cid <- t.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID), mu = 0)
q1c_cid
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
## t = 5.9022, df = 21, p-value = 7.38e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.06586601 0.13753300
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
## 0.1016995
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) # non-parametric, p < 0.05
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## W = 0.93344, p-value = 0.1447
q1c_spr <- t.test(as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR), mu = 0)
q1c_spr
## 
##  One Sample t-test
## 
## data:  as.numeric(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
## t = 5.9327, df = 21, p-value = 6.886e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true mean is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.06916019 0.14381588
## sample estimates:
## mean of x 
##  0.106488

Q1.3 Figures

Q1.3.1 Extant (Figure S1)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create a dataframe
df_extant <- data.frame(
  RFdist = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF),  # Combine the vectors into a single column
  CID = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID),
  SPR = c(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR),
  comparison = rep(c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"), each = length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))  # Repeat vector names
)
df_extant$comparison <- factor(df_extant$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999",
                    "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4",
                    "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)
s1_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=RFdist, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized RF", y=NULL,tag="A")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=CID, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized CID", y=NULL,tag="B")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
s1_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x=SPR, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized SPR", y=NULL,tag="C")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = comparison, y = CID, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
legend_grob <- cowplot::get_legend(legend)
## Warning in get_plot_component(plot, "guide-box"): Multiple components found;
## returning the first one. To return all, use `return_all = TRUE`.
#tiff("Figures/figS1_density.tiff", width = 6, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(s1_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=1),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0466
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0336
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0323

#dev.off()

Q1.3.2 Extant + Extinct (Figure S2)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create a dataframe
df_extinct <- data.frame(
  RFdist = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF),  # Combine the vectors into a single column
  CID = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID),
  SPR = c(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR),
  comparison = rep(c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"), each = length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))  # Repeat vector names
)
df_extinct$comparison <- factor(df_extinct$comparison, levels = c("MPmol_MPte", "MLmol_MLteAsc", "MLmol_MLteNoAsc"))
custom_palette <- c("MPmol_MPte" = "#339999",
                    "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "#6f77b4",
                    "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "#cccccc")
custom_labels <- c(
  "MPmol_MPte" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "MLmol_MLteAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "MLmol_MLteNoAsc" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)
s1_rf = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=RFdist, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized RF", y=NULL,tag="A")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_cid = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=CID, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized CID", y=NULL,tag="B")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
s1_spr = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x=SPR, y=comparison, fill=comparison))+
  geom_density_ridges() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +  # Set lower limit to 0, prevents extrapolation below 0
  theme_ridges()+
  labs(x="Normalized SPR", y=NULL,tag="C")+
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 1, alpha=.5) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Remove y-axis values
    legend.position = "none",     # Move the legend below the plot
    legend.direction = "horizontal", # Display legend items horizontally (side by side)
    legend.box = "horizontal",      # Make sure the legend is laid out horizontally
    legend.title = element_blank(),  # Optional: Remove legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),  # Reduce the size of legend text
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),    # Reduce the size of legend keys
    axis.title.x = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # Center the x-axis title
  )
# Extract the legend as a separate ggplot object
legend <- ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = comparison, y = CID, color = comparison)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  )+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2, alpha=.7)))  # Increase dot size in legend
legend_grob <- cowplot::get_legend(legend)
## Warning in get_plot_component(plot, "guide-box"): Multiple components found;
## returning the first one. To return all, use `return_all = TRUE`.
#tiff("Figures/figS2_density.tiff", width = 6, height = 8, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(s1_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         s1_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=1),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0833
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0649
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0396

#dev.off()

Q2.Are MOL-TE incongruences associated with the proportion of phenomic characters?

Q2.1 Spearman correlations

To test if the proportion of phenomic characters is associated with the topological metrics of MOL-TE incongruences, we used a non-parametric Spearman’s Rank Correlation test. The rho values measure the association between two variables based on their rank orders: a rho of +1 indicates a perfect monotonic increasing relationship; a rho of -1 indicates a perfect monotonic decreasing relationship; a rho of zero indicates a no monotonic relationship.

Q2.1.1 Extant (Table 2)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("RF: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "RF: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF
## S = 14589, p-value = 2.515e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5272004
plot_mp_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized RF\n", x = NULL)

print("CID: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "CID: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID
## S = 13266, p-value = 3.677e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5700664
plot_mp_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized CID\n", x = NULL)

print("SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 14472, p-value = 2.145e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5309872
plot_mp_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.33) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized SPR\n", x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## S = 18526, p-value = 0.002074
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.3995979
plot_mlasc_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID
## S = 17416, p-value = 0.0007079
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4355782
plot_mlasc_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.515) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles
  
print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 17018, p-value = 0.0004676
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4484707
plot_mlasc_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.334) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
## [1] "RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF
## S = 18928, p-value = 0.002976
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##      rho 
## 0.386558
plot_mlno_rf = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
## [1] "CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID
## S = 18957, p-value = 0.003051
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.3856368
plot_mlno_cid = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.525) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles

print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
## [1] "SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)"
cor.test(df_extant$X.Morph.Total, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extant$X.Morph.Total and df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 17423, p-value = 0.0007135
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4353291
plot_mlno_spr = ggplot(df_extant, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.085, label.y = 0.42) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")

Q2.1.2 Extant + extinct (Table 2)

To test if the proportion of phenomic characters is associated with the topological metrics of MOL-TE incongruences, we used a non-parametric Spearman’s Rank Correlation test. The rho values measure the association between two variables based on their rank orders: a rho of +1 indicates a perfect monotonic increasing relationship; a rho of -1 indicates a perfect monotonic decreasing relationship; a rho of zero indicates a no monotonic relationship.

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("RF: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "RF: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF
## S = 913.76, p-value = 0.02245
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4840441
plot_mp_rf_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized RF\n", x = NULL)

print("CID: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "CID: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID
## S = 869.75, p-value = 0.01558
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5088958
plot_mp_cid_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized CID\n", x = NULL)

print("SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 929.05, p-value = 0.02534
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4754101
plot_mp_spr_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.33) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized SPR\n", x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## S = 1018.7, p-value = 0.04876
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4248109
plot_mlasc_rf_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID
## S = 971.74, p-value = 0.03501
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.4513042
plot_mlasc_cid_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
 # stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.515) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Remove x and y axis titles
  
print("SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "SPR: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 835.35, p-value = 0.01149
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5283147
plot_mlasc_spr_fossil = ggplot(df_extinct, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  #stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.334) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = NULL, x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)")
## [1] "RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (noASC)"
cor.test(df_extinct$X.Morph.Total, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_extinct$X.Morph.Total and df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF
## S = 1004.2, p-value = 0.04413

##       rho 
## 0.4329868

  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#cccccc", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend

Q2.1.3 Reduced (Table S12)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_filtered <- df[df$Dataset %in% df_extinct$Dataset, ]
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("RF: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "RF: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_filtered$X.Morph.Total, df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_filtered$X.Morph.Total and df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF
## S = 745.71, p-value = 0.004757
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5789325
plot_mp_rf = ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized RF\n", x = NULL)

print("CID: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "CID: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_filtered$X.Morph.Total, df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_filtered$X.Morph.Total and df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID
## S = 699.7, p-value = 0.002859
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.6049139
plot_mp_cid = ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.6) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized CID\n", x = NULL)

print("SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE")
## [1] "SPR: MP MOL vs MP TE"
cor.test(df_filtered$X.Morph.Total, df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_filtered$X.Morph.Total and df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR
## S = 731.71, p-value = 0.004092
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.5868399
plot_mp_spr = ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#339999", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.08, label.y = 0.33) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(y = "Normalized SPR\n", x = "\n% Phenomic characters")
print("RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "RF: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_filtered$X.Morph.Total, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, method = "spearman")
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  df_filtered$X.Morph.Total and df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## S = 1162.7, p-value = 0.1175
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.3435029
plot_mlasc_rf = ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = X.Morph.Total, y = ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF)) +
  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, color = "#6f77b4", se = FALSE) +  # regression line (lm) for the trend
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 0.09, label.y = 0.73) +  # add Spearman correlation value
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) # Remove x and y axis titles

print("CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)")
## [1] "CID: ML MOL vs ML TE (ASC)"
cor.test(df_filtered$X.Morph.Total, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, method = "spearman")
##

  geom_point() +  # scatter plot
