Q2.2 Figures

Q2.2.1 Extant (Figure 3)

# Grid 3x3
tiff("Figures/fig3_proportion.tiff", width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Q2.2.2 Extant + extinct (Figure S3)

# Grid 3x3
tiff("Figures/figS3_proportion_fossils.tiff", width = 12, height = 10, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(arrangeGrob(plot_mp_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_rf_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_cid_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mp_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlasc_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         plot_mlno_spr_fossil + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
                         nrow=3, ncol=3),
             legend_grob,
             nrow=2,
             heights= c(20,1))
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Q3. Is the addition of phenomic evidence equally impactful on ML and MP topologies?

Q3.1 MOL-TE incongruences ~ Optimality criteria

To test whether the MOL-TE incongruences are different between ML and MP, we first need to change the structure of the dataframe.

Q3.1.1 Extant (Table 3)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create an empty data frame for RF
q3_rf <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of RF into the data frame
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf <- rbind(q3_rf, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF))))
# Summary of medians
q3_rf %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC          0.125    0.153    57
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC        0.12     0.156    57
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE             0.127    0.150    57
# Create an empty data frame for CID
q3_cid <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid <- rbind(q3_cid, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID))))
# Summary of medians
q3_cid %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0922    0.103    57
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0916    0.107    57
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.115     0.135    57
# Create an empty data frame for SPR
q3_spr <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr <- rbind(q3_spr, data.frame(value = df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
# Summary of medians
q3_spr %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.101    0.0825    57
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0948   0.0900    57
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.104    0.0813    57

Then, we need to statistically test whether MOL-TE incongruences (operationally measured as normalized RF, CID, and SPR) differ between (1) MP MOL vs TE, (2) ML MOL vs TE MKv (with bias correction), and (3) ML MOL vs TE MK (no bias correction). Because data are not normally distributed (Shapiro-Wilk tests: P < 0.05) and more than one category is present in the predictor variable, we performed the following tests:

  • The Kruskal-Wallis test is a nonparametric test used to compare the medians (or ranks) between >2 groups. As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations must be independent. First, data is ranked (from the lowest to the highest value) and the mean rank computed. Next, we compute the statistic H (differences between group mean ranks and the overall mean rank, scaled by sample size). Finally, p-values can be estimated by comparing the statistic H and chi-squared distribution (if H is large, the p-value will be small).

  • The Friedman test is a nonparametric test used to compare ordinal or continuous values that are paired between >2 groups. As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations can be dependent (paired). First, ranks are assigned to the pairwise structure across groups. Next, the average rank within group is computed. The Friedman statistic measures how far group rank sums deviate from the expected value in the null model. Finally, p-values are computed by comparing the Friedman statistic and chi-squared distribution.

  • The Wilcoxon signed-rank test is a nonparametric test used to compare the ordinal or continuous values between 2 groups (e.g. as posthoc analysis of Friedman tests). As assumptions, this test does not require normality and observations can be dependent. First, all values are ranked. The number of times that a value from one group precedes a value from the other is computed, generating the U statistic (the number of pairwise wins of one group over the other). Next, U is standardized to a normal distribution to obtain the p-value.

print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(q3_rf$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_rf$value
## W = 0.82726, p-value = 5.956e-13
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)"
kruskal.test(q3_rf$value ~ q3_rf$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_rf$value by q3_rf$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.35512, df = 2, p-value = 0.8373
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_rf_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_rf_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_rf_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 0.19277, df = 2, p-value = 0.9081
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_rf_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_rf_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))) 
## 
##                                        df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF 1                                   
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      1                                   
##                                        df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF -                                     
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      1                                     
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(q3_cid$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_cid$value
## W = 0.80604, p-value = 8.479e-14
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_cid$value ~ q3_cid$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_cid$value by q3_cid$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 3.3268, df = 2, p-value = 0.1895
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_cid_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_cid_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_cid_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 7.8971, df = 2, p-value = 0.01928
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_cid_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_cid_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))) 
## 
##                                         df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID 1.000                                
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.043                                
##                                         df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID -                                      
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.030                                  
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(q3_spr$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_spr$value
## W = 0.92427, p-value = 8.742e-08
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_spr$value ~ q3_spr$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_spr$value by q3_spr$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.55752, df = 2, p-value = 0.7567
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_spr_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extant$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_spr_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_spr_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 0.36735, df = 2, p-value = 0.8322
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_spr_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_spr_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))) 
## 
##                                             df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR 1                                        
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      1                                        
##                                             df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_extant.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR -                                          
## df_extant.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      1                                          
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni

Q3.1.2 Extant + extinct (Table 3)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create an empty data frame for RF
q3_rf_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of RF into the data frame
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_extinct <- rbind(q3_rf_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF))))
# Summary of medians
q3_rf_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0997    0.160    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.100     0.155    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.193     0.288    22
# Create an empty data frame for CID
q3_cid_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_extinct <- rbind(q3_cid_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID))))
# Summary of medians
q3_cid_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0817   0.0928    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0796   0.0913    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.228    0.290     22
# Create an empty data frame for SPR
q3_spr_extinct <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_extinct <- rbind(q3_spr_extinct, data.frame(value = df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
# Summary of medians
q3_spr_extinct %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0878   0.0886    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0878   0.0919    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.0905   0.0799    22
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(q3_rf_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_rf_extinct$value
## W = 0.79674, p-value = 3.877e-08
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)"
kruskal.test(q3_rf_extinct$value ~ q3_rf_extinct$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_rf_extinct$value by q3_rf_extinct$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 5.7297, df = 2, p-value = 0.05699
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_rf_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_rf_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_rf_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 8.7778, df = 2, p-value = 0.01241
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_rf_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_rf_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))) 
## 
##                                         df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF 0.280                                
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      0.038                                
##                                         df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF -                                      
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      0.031                                  
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(q3_cid_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_cid_extinct$value
## W = 0.72511, p-value = 8.351e-10
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_cid_extinct$value ~ q3_cid_extinct$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_cid_extinct$value by q3_cid_extinct$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 10.873, df = 2, p-value = 0.004354
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_cid_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_cid_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_cid_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 12.521, df = 2, p-value = 0.001911
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_cid_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_cid_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))) 
## 
##                                          df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID 0.22778                               
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.00091                               
##                                          df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID -                                       
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.00077                                 
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(q3_spr_extinct$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_spr_extinct$value
## W = 0.92712, p-value = 0.0008166
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_spr_extinct$value ~ q3_spr_extinct$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_spr_extinct$value by q3_spr_extinct$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.060298, df = 2, p-value = 0.9703
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_spr_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_extinct$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_spr_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_spr_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 2.1067, df = 2, p-value = 0.3488
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_spr_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_spr_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))) 
## 
##                                              df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR 0.87                                      
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      1.00                                      
##                                              df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_extinct.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR -                                           
## df_extinct.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      1.00                                        
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni

Q3.1.3 Plot (Figure 4)

# Set order of comparisons
q3_rf$source <- factor(q3_rf$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_cid$source <- factor(q3_cid$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_spr$source <- factor(q3_spr$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))

# Set colors
custom_palette <- c("MP_MOLvsTE" = "#339999", "ML_MOLvsTEASC" = "#6f77b4", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC" = "#cccccc")
# Create custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MP_MOLvsTE" = "MP-MOL vs MP-TE",
  "ML_MOLvsTEASC" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (with ASC)",
  "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC" = "ML-MOL vs ML-TE (without ASC)"
)

# Boxplot Normalized RF
plot_q3_rf = ggplot(q3_rf, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "Normalized RF\n", tag="A") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot CID
plot_q3_cid = ggplot(q3_cid, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nNormalized CID\n", tag="B") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot SPR
plot_q3_spr = ggplot(q3_spr, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "\nSPR moves\n", tag="C") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none", axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.title = element_blank())+
#theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
q3_rf_extinct$source <- factor(q3_rf_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_cid_extinct$source <- factor(q3_cid_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))
q3_spr_extinct$source <- factor(q3_spr_extinct$source, levels = c("MP_MOLvsTE", "ML_MOLvsTEASC", "ML_MOLvsTEnoASC"))

# Boxplot Normalized RF
plot_q3_rf_extinct = ggplot(q3_rf_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="D") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot CID
plot_q3_cid_extinct = ggplot(q3_cid_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="E") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Boxplot SPR
plot_q3_spr_extinct = ggplot(q3_spr_extinct, aes(x = source, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_jitter(aes(color = source), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "", tag="F") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none", axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),legend.title = element_blank())+
#theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),legend.position = "none") +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette, labels = custom_labels)
# Plot 3x1
tiff("Figures/fig4_boxplots.tiff", width = 5, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
grid.arrange(plot_q3_rf + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_rf_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.01,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_cid + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_cid_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_spr + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             plot_q3_spr_extinct + theme(plot.margin = margin(.001,1,1,.01)),
             nrow=3, ncol=2)
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Q3.1.4 Reduced (Table S13)

Considering only extant taxa

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_filtered <- df[df$Dataset %in% df_filtered$Dataset, ]
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Create an empty data frame for RF
q3_rf_reduced <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of RF into the data frame
q3_rf_reduced <- rbind(q3_rf_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_reduced <- rbind(q3_rf_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF))))
q3_rf_reduced <- rbind(q3_rf_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF))))
# Summary of medians
q3_rf_reduced %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC          0.122    0.137    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC        0.116    0.140    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE             0.124    0.147    22
# Create an empty data frame for CID
q3_cid_reduced <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_cid_reduced <- rbind(q3_cid_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_reduced <- rbind(q3_cid_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID))))
q3_cid_reduced <- rbind(q3_cid_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID))))
# Summary of medians
q3_cid_reduced %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0942   0.0799    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0942   0.0808    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.113    0.114     22
# Create an empty data frame for SPR
q3_spr_reduced <- data.frame(value = numeric(0), source = character(0))
# Combine the vectors of CID into the data frame
q3_spr_reduced <- rbind(q3_spr_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("MP_MOLvsTE", length(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_reduced <- rbind(q3_spr_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
q3_spr_reduced <- rbind(q3_spr_reduced, data.frame(value = df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, source = rep("ML_MOLvsTEnoASC", length(df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR))))
# Summary of medians
q3_spr_reduced %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(
    median_value = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_value = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n())
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   source          median_value sd_value     n
##   <chr>                  <dbl>    <dbl> <int>
## 1 ML_MOLvsTEASC         0.0978   0.0824    22
## 2 ML_MOLvsTEnoASC       0.0923   0.0842    22
## 3 MP_MOLvsTE            0.127    0.0862    22
print("RF")
## [1] "RF"
shapiro.test(q3_rf_reduced$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_rf_reduced$value
## W = 0.87474, p-value = 7.676e-06
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups (non-paired values)"
kruskal.test(q3_rf_reduced$value ~ q3_rf_reduced$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_rf_reduced$value by q3_rf_reduced$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.37406, df = 2, p-value = 0.8294
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_rf_fri = data.frame(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_rf_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_rf_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 0.62687, df = 2, p-value = 0.7309
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_rf_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_rf_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_rf_fri), each = nrow(q3_rf_fri))) 
## 
##                                          df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedRF
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF 1                                     
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      1                                     
##                                          df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedRF -                                       
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedRF      1                                       
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("CID")
## [1] "CID"
shapiro.test(q3_cid_reduced$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_cid_reduced$value
## W = 0.92089, p-value = 0.0004379
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_cid_reduced$value ~ q3_cid_reduced$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_cid_reduced$value by q3_cid_reduced$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.80687, df = 2, p-value = 0.668
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_cid_fri = data.frame(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_cid_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_cid_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 7.4118, df = 2, p-value = 0.02458
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_cid_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_cid_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_cid_fri), each = nrow(q3_cid_fri))) 
## 
##                                           df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedCID
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID 1.000                                  
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.065                                  
##                                           df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedCID -                                        
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedCID      0.065                                    
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
print("SPR")
## [1] "SPR"
shapiro.test(q3_spr_reduced$value) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  q3_spr_reduced$value
## W = 0.94136, p-value = 0.003695
print("Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups")
## [1] "Kruskal-Wallis: non-parametric test for multiple groups"
kruskal.test(q3_spr_reduced$value ~ q3_spr_reduced$source)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  q3_spr_reduced$value by q3_spr_reduced$source
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.54658, df = 2, p-value = 0.7609
print("Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups")
## [1] "Friedman: non-parametric test for paired values and multiple groups"
q3_spr_fri = data.frame(df_filtered$MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR, df_filtered$ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_spr_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_spr_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 2.7143, df = 2, p-value = 0.2574
print("Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values")
## [1] "Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values"
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_spr_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_spr_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_spr_fri), each = nrow(q3_spr_fri))) 
## 
##                                               df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR 1.00                                       
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      0.77                                       
##                                               df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR
## df_filtered.ML_MOLvsTEnoASC_NormalizedMeanSPR -                                            
## df_filtered.MP_MOLvsTE_NormalizedMeanSPR      0.60                                         
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni

Q3.2 Number of polytomies

One importance difference, however, is the fact that trees were always binary in ML MOL and ML TE, whereas the addition of phenomic evidence can maintain, increase or decrease resolution of MP trees.

Q3.2.1 Extant

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL"
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) > 0)
## [1] 16
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE"
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) < 0)
## [1] 15
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE"
sum((df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) == 0)
## [1] 26
cat("\n")
print("MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies"
mean(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 3
print("MP MOL: SD no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: SD no. polytomies"
sd(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 5.365232
print("MP MOL: Min no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Min no. polytomies"
min(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 0
print("MP MOL: Max no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Max no. polytomies"
max(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 31
print("MP TE: Mean no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Mean no. polytomies"
mean(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 2.614035
print("MP TE: SD no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: SD no. polytomies"
sd(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 4.566002
print("MP TE: Min no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Min no. polytomies"
min(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 0
print("MP TE: Max no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Max no. polytomies"
max(df_extant$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 30

Q3.2.2 Extant + extinct

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in TE than MOL"
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) > 0)
## [1] 17
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is higher in MOL than TE"
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) < 0)
## [1] 2
print("MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE")
## [1] "MP: Number of datasets in which the number of politomies is equal in MOL and TE"
sum((df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL) == 0)
## [1] 3
cat("\n")
print("MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Mean no. polytomies"
mean(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 1.5
print("MP MOL: SD no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: SD no. polytomies"
sd(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 2.558832
print("MP MOL: Min no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Min no. polytomies"
min(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 0
print("MP MOL: Max no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP MOL: Max no. polytomies"
max(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 11
print("MP TE: Mean no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Mean no. polytomies"
mean(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 3.818182
print("MP TE: SD no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: SD no. polytomies"
sd(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 3.049377
print("MP TE: Min no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Min no. polytomies"
min(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 0
print("MP TE: Max no. polytomies")
## [1] "MP TE: Max no. polytomies"
max(df_extinct$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 11

Q3.2.3 Statistical tests (Figure S4; Table S6)

Is the difference between MOL and TE resolution higher when fossils are incorporated?

# Load data
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
# 1. Filter rows where Dataset is duplicated
df_filtered <- df %>%
  filter(Dataset %in% Dataset[duplicated(Dataset)])
# 2. Keep only the relevant columns
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>%
  select(Type, MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE)
# 3. Compute paired difference
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>%
  mutate(diff = MP_nPolytomiesTE - MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
# 4. Reshape
df_extant <- df_filtered %>% filter(Type == "Extant")
df_extinct <- df_filtered %>% filter(Type == "Extinct")
n_extant <- nrow(df_extant)
df_extinct <- df_extinct %>% slice(1:n_extant)
df_extinct <- df_extinct %>%
  select(-Type) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_Extinct"))
df_wide <- cbind(df_extant, df_extinct)
df_wide <- df_wide %>% select(-Type)
# Exclude MP_nPolytomiesMOL_Extinct
df_reduced <- df_wide %>%
  select(-MP_nPolytomiesMOL_Extinct)

# 5. Exploratory (Table S5)
print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
## [1] "Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils"
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 0
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
## [1] "SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils"
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 1.432462
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
## [1] "Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils"
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] -4
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils")
## [1] "Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (without fossils"
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 3
print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils"
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 1
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils"
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 3.014036
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils"
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] -3
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP MOL and MP TE (with fossils"
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesMOL)
## [1] 10
print("Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Median of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils"
median(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 1
print("SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "SD of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils"
sd(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 2.663282
print("Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Min of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils"
min(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] -1
print("Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils")
## [1] "Max of paired differences in number of polytomies between MP TE (without fossils) and MP TE (with fossils"
max(df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct - df_reduced$MP_nPolytomiesTE)
## [1] 10
# 6. Statistical inference (Table S5)
df_friedman <- df_wide %>% 
  select(MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE, MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct)
# Friedman test
friedman_test <- friedman.test(as.matrix(df_friedman))
friedman_test
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(df_friedman)
## Friedman chi-squared = 18.627, df = 2, p-value = 9.02e-05
# Posthoc Wilcoxon
# Add row ID for pairing
df_plot <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())
# Convert to long format
df_long <- df_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE, MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct),
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "value"
  )
pairwise.wilcox.test(df_long$value, df_long$group, 
                     paired = TRUE, 
                     p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with zeroes
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with zeroes
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with zeroes
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  df_long$value and df_long$group 
## 
##                          MP_nPolytomiesMOL MP_nPolytomiesTE
## MP_nPolytomiesTE         0.4854            -               
## MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct 0.0046            0.0040          
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
# 7. FIGURE S4
# Add row ID for pairing
df_plot <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number())
# Convert to long format
df_long <- df_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(MP_nPolytomiesMOL, MP_nPolytomiesTE, MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct),
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "value"
  )
# Create a factor to order the groups in plotting
df_long$group <- factor(df_long$group, levels = c("MP_nPolytomiesMOL", 
                                                  "MP_nPolytomiesTE",
                                                  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct"))
# Plot paired dots with connecting lines
custom_colors <- c(
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE" = "#339999",         # lightest
  "MP_nPolytomiesMOL" = "#66CCCC",        # light
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct" = "#006666" # darker
)
# Custom legend labels
custom_labels <- c(
  "MP_nPolytomiesMOL" = "MOL (without fossils)",
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE" = "TE (without fossils)",
  "MP_nPolytomiesTE_Extinct" = "TE (with fossils)"
)
# Plot with gray lines, colored dots, renamed legend, no x-axis
p = ggplot(df_long, aes(x = group, y = value, group = row_id)) +
  geom_line(color = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(color = group), size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of Polytomies\n", 
       title = "") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, labels = custom_labels, name=NULL)
p

# Save the plot as JPG, 300 dpi
ggsave(filename = "Figures/figS4_polytomies_MP.jpg", 
       plot = p, 
       width = 8, height = 5,      # size in inches
       dpi = 300,                   # resolution
       units = "in")

Q3.3 Contribution of phenomic evidence to the TE optimality score

In addition to testing whether MOL-TE incongruences differ between optimaliy criteria, we can explore the impact of phenomic evidence to optimality scores between optimality criteria. Specifically, it is possible that the MKv model can constrain the contribution of the phenomic partition to the TE optimality score in ML, whereas the same does not occur in MP analyses. As such, we need to test whether the proportion of optimality scores between MORPH and TE is higher in MP than ML analyses.

Q3.3.1 Extant

# Load data for extant taxa
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extant <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extant", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Exploratory data analyses
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC"
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 32
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC"
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 25
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC"
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 31
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC"
sum(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 26
print("Stats MP MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats MP MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.07503085
sd(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.07966847
min(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.000389759
max(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.4081309
print("Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.06260997
sd(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.06369744
min(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 5.23e-05
max(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.3929691
print("Stats ML MK MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats ML MK MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.0642674
sd(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.06439678
min(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 5.25e-05
max(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.3937384
# Normality tests
shapiro.test(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score
## W = 0.76725, p-value = 4.029e-08
shapiro.test(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score
## W = 0.75212, p-value = 1.887e-08
shapiro.test(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score
## W = 0.76455, p-value = 3.511e-08
# Friedman test (non-parametric, multiple groups, paired values)
q3_fri = data.frame(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 20.414, df = 2, p-value = 3.691e-05
# Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))) 
## 
##                                  df_extant.ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score
## df_extant.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score 4.8e-08                       
## df_extant.MP_MORPH.TE_Score      0.34                          
##                                  df_extant.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score
## df_extant.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score -                               
## df_extant.MP_MORPH.TE_Score      0.79                            
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
# Figure
values <- c(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
group <- c(rep("MP", length(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)),
             rep("ML MKV", length(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)),
             rep("ML MK", length(df_extant$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)))
block = rep(1:length(df_extant$MP_MORPH.TE_Score), 3)  # Indices (same length as vectors)
df_nemenyi <- data.frame(value = values, group = group, block=block)
df_nemenyi$group <- factor(df_nemenyi$group, levels = c("MP", "ML MKV", "ML MK"))
custom_palette <- c("MP" = "#339999", "ML MKV" = "#6f77b4", "ML MK" = "#cccccc")

contribution_extant = ggplot(df_nemenyi, aes(x = group, y = value, group = block)) +
  #geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_line(aes(group = block), color = "black", alpha = 0.1) +  # Link paired values
  geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "% Phenomic/TE optimality score\n") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette)
## Warning in geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2): Ignoring
## unknown parameters: `width`

Q3.3.2 Extant + extinct

# Load data for extant taxa
df <- read.csv("Dataset_R_q123_08-25.csv")
df_extinct <- df %>% filter(grepl("Extinct", Type)) # keep only extant datasets

# Exploratory data analyses
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML ASC"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 10
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML ASC"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 12
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is higher in MP than ML noASC"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score > df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 10
print("Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC")
## [1] "Number of times that %MORPH/TE is lower in MP than ML noASC"
sum(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score < df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 12
print("Stats MP MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats MP MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.08229641
sd(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.07988527
min(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.000605816
max(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)
## [1] 0.3209799
print("Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats ML MKV MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.07290503
sd(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.05783672
min(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.00067116
max(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)
## [1] 0.2069809
print("Stats ML MK MORPH/TE")
## [1] "Stats ML MK MORPH/TE"
mean(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.07400197
sd(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.05861277
min(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.000674379
max(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
## [1] 0.215919
# Normality tests
shapiro.test(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score) # p < 0.05, non-parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score
## W = 0.86139, p-value = 0.005434
shapiro.test(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score) # p > 0.05, parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score
## W = 0.91232, p-value = 0.05282
shapiro.test(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score) # p > 0.05, parametric
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score
## W = 0.91532, p-value = 0.06086
# Friedman test (non-parametric, multiple groups, paired values)
q3_fri = data.frame(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
friedman.test(as.matrix(q3_fri))
## 
##  Friedman rank sum test
## 
## data:  as.matrix(q3_fri)
## Friedman chi-squared = 4.8372, df = 2, p-value = 0.08905
# Wilcoxon: pairwise comparisons for paired values
pairwise.wilcox.test(x=unlist(q3_fri),
                     g=factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))),
                     paired=TRUE,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni")
## Warning in wilcox.test.default(xi, xj, paired = paired, ...): cannot compute
## exact p-value with zeroes
## 
##  Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction 
## 
## data:  unlist(q3_fri) and factor(rep(names(q3_fri), each = nrow(q3_fri))) 
## 
##                                   df_extinct.ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score
## df_extinct.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score 0.0029                         
## df_extinct.MP_MORPH.TE_Score      1.0000                         
##                                   df_extinct.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score
## df_extinct.ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score -                                
## df_extinct.MP_MORPH.TE_Score      1.0000                           
## 
## P value adjustment method: bonferroni
# Figure
values <- c(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score, df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)
group <- c(rep("MP", length(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score)),
             rep("ML MKV", length(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TeASC_Score)),
             rep("ML MK", length(df_extinct$ML_MORPH.TEnoASC_Score)))
block = rep(1:length(df_extinct$MP_MORPH.TE_Score), 3)  # Indices (same length as vectors)
df_nemenyi <- data.frame(value = values, group = group, block=block)
df_nemenyi$group <- factor(df_nemenyi$group, levels = c("MP", "ML MKV", "ML MK"))
custom_palette <- c("MP" = "#339999", "ML MKV" = "#6f77b4", "ML MK" = "#cccccc")

contribution_extinct = ggplot(df_nemenyi, aes(x = group, y = value, group = block)) +
  #geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +  # Create boxplot without default outliers
  geom_line(aes(group = block), color = "black", alpha = 0.1) +  # Link paired values
  geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = .4, width = 0.2) +  # Add transparent dots
  labs(x = "", y = "% Phenomic/TE optimality score\n") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank()) +  # Hide legend if not needed
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_palette)
## Warning in geom_point(aes(color = group), alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2): Ignoring
## unknown parameters: `width`

Q3.3.3 Plot (Figure S5)

s5= plot_grid(contribution_extant, 
          contribution_extinct, 
          labels = c("A", "B"),   # adds panel labels
          ncol = 1)               # A on top of B
# Save to JPG at 300 dpi
ggsave("Figures/figS5_contributionPhenomic.jpg", s5, dpi = 300,
       width = 6, height = 8, units = "in")  # adjust size

Q4. How do bootstrap values of shared clades differ between MOL and TE trees?

Q4.1 Unique vs Shared

Q4.2 With and without fossils (Table 1 and S7, Figure 6)

Before testing the differences of BS values in shared clades between MOL and TE trees, let’s explore the differences of BS values between shared and unique clades.

First, we need to format the extant datasets.

# Load shared clades
df_shared = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared.csv")
# Load unique clades
df_unique = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_unique.csv")

# Rename the column Analysis to Dataset
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Dataset = Analysis)
# Rename the column Method to Optimality
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Optimality = Method)
# Rename the column Type to Analysis
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Analysis = Type)
# Create a new column called Type (unique vs shared)
df_unique <- df_unique %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "unique")


# Create a new dataframe from df_shared with the selected columns
df_shared_stack <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "MOL",                 # new value
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_1,               # take Node_Tree_1
    Support = Support_Tree_1,         # take Support_Tree_1
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Bind the new rows below df_unique
df_unique_extended <- bind_rows(df_unique, df_shared_stack)
# Create a new dataframe from df_shared for Tree 2
df_shared_stack2 <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "TE",                  # new value for Tree 2
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_2,               # take Node_Tree_2
    Support = Support_Tree_2,         # take Support_Tree_2
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Append Tree 2 rows to df_unique_extended (which already contains Tree 1 and unique rows)
df_uniqueVSshared <- bind_rows(df_unique_extended, df_shared_stack2)

# Rename
df_uniqueVSshared <- df_uniqueVSshared %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML-ASC" = "ML_ASC",
      "ML-noASC" = "ML_noASC"
    )
  )
# Add the column Sampling (without fossils vs with fossils)
df_uniqueVSshared <- df_uniqueVSshared %>%
  mutate(Sampling = "Without_fossils")

#View(df_uniqueVSshared)

Now, we need to format the datasets including fossils.

# Load shared clades
df_shared = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_shared_extinct.csv")
# Load unique clades
df_unique = read.csv ("/Users/labanfibios/Desktop/Doutorado/Project/B2_TEvsMOL/GitHub/Dataset_R_q4_09-25_unique_extinct.csv")

# Rename the column Analysis to Dataset
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Dataset = Analysis)
# Rename the column Method to Optimality
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Optimality = Method)
# Rename the column Type to Analysis
df_unique <- df_unique %>% 
  rename(Analysis = Type)
# Create a new column called Type (unique vs shared)
df_unique <- df_unique %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = "unique")

# Create a new dataframe from df_shared with the selected columns
df_shared_stack <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "MOL",                 # new value
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_1,               # take Node_Tree_1
    Support = Support_Tree_1,         # take Support_Tree_1
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Bind the new rows below df_unique
df_unique_extended <- bind_rows(df_unique, df_shared_stack)
# Create a new dataframe from df_shared for Tree 2
df_shared_stack2 <- df_shared %>%
  transmute(
    Dataset = Dataset,
    Analysis = "TE",                  # new value for Tree 2
    Optimality = Optimality,
    Node = Node_Tree_2,               # take Node_Tree_2
    Support = Support_Tree_2,         # take Support_Tree_2
    Type = "shared"                   # new value
  )
# Append Tree 2 rows to df_unique_extended (which already contains Tree 1 and unique rows)
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- bind_rows(df_unique_extended, df_shared_stack2)

# Rename
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- df_uniqueVSshared_extinct %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML-ASC" = "ML_ASC",
      "ML-noASC" = "ML_noASC"
    )
  )
# Add column Sampling (with vs without fossils)
df_uniqueVSshared_extinct <- df_uniqueVSshared_extinct %>%
  mutate(Sampling = "With_fossils")
#View(df_uniqueVSshared_extinct)

The dataframes are formatted. Now, we can combine them, perform exploratory data analyses, and plot them.

# Combine dataframes with and without fossils
df_combined <- bind_rows(df_uniqueVSshared, df_uniqueVSshared_extinct)
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(Optimality = recode(Optimality,
    "ML_ASC"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML MKv"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML MKV"   = "ML_MKv",
    "ML_noASC" = "ML_MK",
    "ML MK"    = "ML_MK"
  ))
#View(df_combined)

# Exploratory data analysis
q4_explore = df_combined %>%
  group_by(Analysis, Optimality, Type, Sampling) %>%
  summarise(
    min_Support = min(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_Support = max(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_Support = mean(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_Support = sd(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#View(q4_explore)

# Rename Optimality
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(
    Optimality = recode(
      Optimality,
      "ML_ASC" = "ML MKV",
      "ML_noASC" = "ML MK"
    )
  )

# Create a combined factor for coloring
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(
    ColorGroup = paste(Optimality, Type, Analysis, sep = "_")
  )

# common aesthetics / colors
fill_colors <- c("shared" = "#4d4d4d", "unique" = "#d9d9d9")

# Plot A: Without_fossils
pA <- df_combined %>%
  filter(Sampling == "Without_fossils") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Analysis, y = Support, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Optimality) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Bootstrap\n", fill = "Node type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Plot B: With_fossils
pB <- df_combined %>%
  filter(Sampling == "With_fossils") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Analysis, y = Support, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Optimality) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Bootstrap\n", fill = "Node type") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Arrange side-by-side with labels A and B in upper-left
figure <- ggarrange(
  pA, pB,
  ncol = 1, nrow = 2,
  common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom",
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  label.x = 0.0,        # x position of labels (left)
  label.y = 0.98,        # y position of labels (top)
  font.label = list(size = 14)
)
figure

#ggsave("Figures/Fig6_BS_uniqueVSshared.jpg", plot = figure, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)

We can statistically test whether bootstrap values differ between shared and unique clades, considering Type (categorical predictor: unique vs shared), Sampling (categorical predictor: Without_fossils vs With_fossils), Optimality (categorical predictor: MP vs ML_ASC vs ML_noASC), Analysis (categorical predictor: MOL vs TE). We will use both traditional non-parametric tests and GLMs.

# Fix: Squeeze Support into (0,1) for GLMM with Beta distribution
df_combined <- df_combined %>%
  mutate(Support_beta = (Support + 0.5) / 101) 

# Split datasets
df_without <- df_combined %>% filter(Sampling == "Without_fossils")
df_with    <- df_combined %>% filter(Sampling == "With_fossils")

# --- Helper function for classical non-parametric tests ---
run_nonparametric_tests <- function(df) {
  results <- list()
  
  # 1. Kruskal-Wallis for Type, Optimality, Analysis
  results$kw_type <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Type)
  results$kw_opt  <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Optimality)
  results$kw_an   <- kruskal_test(df, Support ~ Analysis)
  
  # 2. Pairwise Wilcoxon for Type by Optimality & Analysis
  pw_list <- list()
  
  for(opt in unique(df$Optimality)){
    for(an in unique(df$Analysis)){
      subset_df <- df %>% filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == an)
      test_res <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
        subset_df,
        Support ~ Type,
        p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
      )
      test_res <- test_res %>% mutate(Optimality = opt, Analysis = an)
      pw_list[[paste(opt, an, sep = "_")]] <- test_res
    }
  }
  
  results$pairwise <- bind_rows(pw_list)
  return(results)
}

# Function to summarize mean, sd, min, max
summarize_support <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Optimality, Analysis, Type) %>%
    summarise(
      n    = n(),
      mean = mean(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd   = sd(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      min  = min(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      max  = max(Support, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}


# All combinations to test
combinations <- expand.grid(
  Optimality = c("MP", "ML_MK", "ML_MKv"),
  Analysis = c("MOL", "TE"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Function to run pairwise GLMM for one subset
run_pairwise <- function(df_subset) {
  results <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(combinations)) {
    opt <- combinations$Optimality[i]
    ana <- combinations$Analysis[i]
    
    df_sub <- df_subset %>%
      filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == ana)
    
    if (nrow(df_sub) > 0) {
      # Fit beta GLMM
      glmm <- glmmTMB(
        Support_beta ~ Type + (1|Dataset),
        family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
        data = df_sub
      )
      
      # Pairwise comparisons for Type
      em <- emmeans(glmm, ~ Type)
      pw <- pairs(em, adjust = "bonferroni")
      
      results[[paste(opt, ana, sep = "_")]] <- summary(pw)
    }
  }
  
  bind_rows(results, .id = "Comparison")
}

run_pairwise_df <- function(df_subset, combinations_df, rand = "(1|Dataset)") {
  out_list <- list()
  for(i in seq_len(nrow(combinations_df))) {
    opt <- combinations_df$Optimality[i]
    ana <- combinations_df$Analysis[i]
    df_sub <- df_subset %>% filter(Optimality == opt, Analysis == ana)
    
    # need at least two Type levels and some rows
    if(nrow(df_sub) < 4 || length(unique(df_sub$Type)) < 2) {
      warning(sprintf("Skipping %s_%s: insufficient data (n=%d, types=%s)",
                      opt, ana, nrow(df_sub), paste(unique(df_sub$Type), collapse=", ")))
      next
    }
    
    # Fit model safely
    glmm <- try(
      glmmTMB(Support_beta ~ Type + (1|Dataset), data = df_sub, family = beta_family(link="logit")),
      silent = TRUE
    )
    if(inherits(glmm, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("Model failed for %s_%s: %s", opt, ana, attr(glmm, "condition")$message))
      next
    }
    
    # emmeans and pairwise contrasts
    em <- try(emmeans(glmm, ~ Type), silent = TRUE)
    if(inherits(em, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("emmeans failed for %s_%s", opt, ana))
      next
    }
    pw <- try(pairs(em, adjust = "bonferroni"), silent = TRUE)
    if(inherits(pw, "try-error")) {
      warning(sprintf("pairs() failed for %s_%s", opt, ana))
      next
    }
    
    # convert to data.frame to get exact numeric p-values
    pw_df <- as.data.frame(pw)
    # add context columns
    pw_df <- pw_df %>%
      mutate(
        Optimality = opt,
        Analysis   = ana,
        Comparison = rownames(pw_df)  # usually "unique - shared" or similar
      ) %>%
      select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, everything())
    
    out_list[[paste(opt, ana, sep = "_")]] <- pw_df
  }
  
  if(length(out_list) == 0) return(tibble()) 
  res <- bind_rows(out_list, .id = "id")
  
  # Add nicely formatted p-values:
  res <- res %>%
    mutate(
      p.value_numeric = as.numeric(p.value),
      p.value_fmt = ifelse(
        is.na(p.value_numeric), NA,
        ifelse(p.value_numeric < 0.001,
               formatC(p.value_numeric, format = "e", digits = 2),           # scientific for <0.001
               formatC(p.value_numeric, format = "f", digits = 3))           # 3 decimals otherwise
    ))
  
  return(res)
}
# Table S7

print("Mean, sd, and range of BS without fossils")
## [1] "Mean, sd, and range of BS without fossils"
summary_without <- summarize_support(df_without)
summary_without
## # A tibble: 12 × 8

##    <chr>      <chr>    <chr>  <int> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <int>
##  1 ML_MK      MOL      shared  2676  95.2 10.3     50   100
##  2 ML_MK      MOL      unique   346  78.4 16.1     50   100
##  3 ML_MK      TE       shared  2676  96.2  8.94    50   100
##  4 ML_MK      TE       unique   360  80.0 16.4     50   100
##  5 ML_MKv     MOL      shared  2698  95.0 10.4     50   100
##  6 ML_MKv     MOL      unique   335  78.1 15.9     50   100
##  7 ML_MKv     TE       shared  2698  96.1  9.13    50   100
##  8 ML_MKv     TE       unique   354  79.3 16.2     50   100
##  9 MP         MOL      shared  2279  90.0 14.2     50   100
## 10 MP         MOL      unique   147  62.8 12.3     50   100
## 11 MP         TE       shared  2279  91.0 13.6     50   100
## 12 MP         TE       unique   201  66.3 13.3     50   100
results_without <- run_nonparametric_tests(df_without)
print("Non-parametric Wilcoxon tests: Unique vs Shared BS (without fossils)")
## [1] "Non-parametric Wilcoxon tests: Unique vs Shared BS (without fossils)"
results_without$pairwise

print("GLMM with Beta distribution (without fossils")

# Run for Without_fossils
pairwise_without_df <- run_pairwise_df(df_without, combinations)
# View results: numeric and formatted p-values

  select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, estimate, SE, df, p.value_fmt)

  select(Optimality, Analysis, Comparison, estimate, SE, df, p.value_fmt)
